title: leptodactylon pungens subsp. pulchriflorum
intro: Leptodactylon pungens subsp. pulchriflorum, also known as Pink Mountain Gilga, is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Leptodactylon pungens subsp. pulchriflorum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
